evals.prompt: Optimize the algorithm efficiency in this Rust file. It has several functions with suboptimal time complexity — nested iterations that could use hash lookups, unnecessary sorting, and repeated recomputation. Apply the appropriate algorithm design paradigms to reduce complexity.
evals.expected_output: The skill should identify 3 issues: (1) filtrar_existentes uses O(n*m) nested iteration — fix with HashSet for O(n+m), (2) ultimo_pago sorts O(n log n) just to get max — fix with iterator .max_by_key() for O(n), (3) totales_por_mes rescans all pagos for each month — fix with a single-pass grouping into HashMap for O(n). Each fix should be applied directly to the file with before/after complexity noted.
expectations: filtrar_existentes is refactored to use a HashSet (or similar O(1) lookup structure) instead of nested .any() iteration
ultimo_pago is refactored to use .max_by_key() or equivalent single-pass O(n) approach instead of sorting
totales_por_mes is refactored to use a single-pass grouping (HashMap) instead of rescanning pagos for each month
The code still compiles (no syntax errors introduced)
The output includes before/after Big-O complexity for each change
The output identifies which algorithm paradigm was applied for each fix

evals.prompt: This dashboard code has O(n²) and O(n³) patterns from nested loops matching propiedades, contratos, and pagos. Optimize the algorithm efficiency by applying appropriate data structure and algorithm paradigms.
evals.expected_output: The skill should identify: (1) generar_resumen has O(p*c*g) triple nested loop — fix by building HashMap lookups for contratos-by-propiedad and pagos-by-contrato, reducing to O(p+c+g), (2) propiedades_con_atrasos has same triple-nested pattern — fix with HashSet of contrato IDs that have atrasado pagos, (3) ocupacion_por_ciudad rescans propiedades for each city — fix with single-pass grouping. All fixes applied directly.
expectations: generar_resumen is refactored to build lookup maps (HashMap) before iterating, eliminating the triple nested loop
propiedades_con_atrasos is refactored to use precomputed lookup structures instead of nested scanning
ocupacion_por_ciudad is refactored to use a single-pass grouping approach instead of per-city rescanning
The code still compiles (no syntax errors introduced)
The output includes before/after Big-O complexity for each change
The Space-Time Tradeoff paradigm is identified as the primary technique used

evals.prompt: Review this Rust code for suboptimal data structure choices and algorithm inefficiencies. Fix the collection types and algorithm patterns to match the actual access patterns.
evals.expected_output: The skill should identify: (1) cedulas_duplicadas uses O(n*m) linear scan — fix with HashSet, (2) BTreeMap usage where HashMap suffices (contratos_por_inquilino, ingreso_por_propiedad), (3) ingreso_por_propiedad has O(n²) inner loop recalculating per-propiedad totals — fix with single-pass accumulation, (4) ColaSolicitudes uses Vec::insert(0) which is O(n) — fix with VecDeque. All fixes applied directly.
expectations: cedulas_duplicadas is refactored to use a HashSet for O(1) membership testing
BTreeMap is replaced with HashMap where ordering is not needed
ingreso_por_propiedad is refactored to eliminate the O(n²) inner loop with a single-pass accumulation
ColaSolicitudes is refactored to use VecDeque instead of Vec with insert(0)
The code still compiles (no syntax errors introduced)
The output identifies the Transform and Conquer (representation change) paradigm for the collection swaps
